linting.notes: Pre-commit hooks include: ruff lint + format, pyright type checking, YAML validation, trailing whitespace, end-of-file-fixer, and license header insertion. Ruff is configured for line length 120, excludes common directories, enables F/E/W/I rules. Pyright type checks specific directories only (see pyproject.toml).

testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest fixtures, aioresponses for async HTTP mocking, pytest-httpx for httpx mocking
testing.notes: Tests use pytest with asyncio support (pytest-asyncio). Config in pytest.ini and pyproject.toml. Tests are comprehensive but some require specific test configs. The conftest.py includes auto-use fixtures for resetting reasoning trace variables. Tests run with -p no:warnings flag by default. Test execution takes ~60 seconds for full suite.

gating_checks.name: GitLab CI Tests
gating_checks.command: make test (which runs poetry run pytest tests/)

ci.notes: GitHub Actions runs lint and tests in parallel. Tests run with coverage reporting on Python 3.11. GitLab CI uses Poetry 1.8.2 and caches .venv. Tekton pipeline builds container images for OpenShift on push to develop branch. Pre-commit hooks must pass for lint check. Tests have path-ignore for markdown and .github files.

build.notes: Project uses Poetry for dependency management. Lock file (poetry.lock) must be in sync with pyproject.toml - may need 'poetry lock --no-update' if out of sync. Dev dependencies include pytest, ruff, pyright, pre-commit. Optional extras include: sdd, eval, openai, gcp, tracing, nvidia, jailbreak, models, multilingual, server, all. Main package is 'nemoguardrails' installed in editable mode.

conventions.test_naming: Functions: test_* with descriptive snake_case names
conventions.import_style: Absolute imports preferred. Ruff enforces import sorting (I001, I002 rules). Line length 120 characters.
conventions.code_style: Ruff formatting (like Black): double quotes, 4-space indents, 120 char line length
conventions.type_checking: Pyright used on select directories only. Not full project coverage.
conventions.notes: License headers required on all .py files (Apache 2.0). Pre-commit hook auto-inserts. YAML files validated. Trailing whitespace and EOF fixers enforced. Test fixtures use autouse for state cleanup.

Exit code 1. Found 3 files needing reformatting (scripts/*.py). Fixable with ruff format.

test_commands.notes: Exit code 1. 1741 passed, 5 failed, 70 skipped in 62.03s. Failures due to missing test_configs/simple_rails directory. Test suite is functional.
Coverage reporting works. 12% overall coverage from test subset.

container_recipe.notes: Lock file was out of sync and required 'poetry lock --no-update' before install. This is a common issue when pyproject.toml changes. System dependencies (make, git, curl) are pre-installed in python:3.11 image. Poetry install takes ~5-10 minutes due to large dependency tree. Test execution for full suite takes ~60-120 seconds. Some tests fail due to missing test config directories but overall test infrastructure is solid.

notes: This is a well-structured Python project with comprehensive linting (ruff, pyright) and extensive testing (pytest with ~2794 tests). The main caveats are: (1) poetry lock file may need regeneration if out of sync, (2) a few tests fail due to missing test config directories, and (3) the test suite is very large so execution takes time. An agent can successfully validate patches by running lint and a subset of tests, but should be aware of the lock file sync issue and the few missing test configs. The project uses Poetry for dependency management, supports Python 3.10-3.13, and has CI on both GitHub Actions and GitLab CI.
